title: James Webb Space Telescope maps our universe's largest structure in unprecedented detail
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to map the 'cosmic web' of galaxies, the largest structure in the universe, with unprecedented detail. This breakthrough provides new insights into the large-scale structure of the cosmos.

title: We still can't see dark matter. But what if we can hear it?
summary: Scientists propose that dark matter could be detected through gravitational waves from black hole collisions, comparing the process to churning butter. This theoretical approach offers a new method to indirectly observe the elusive dark matter.

title: The 'Doctor Who' TV movie at 30: Too British for America, too American for the UK
summary: A retrospective on the 30th anniversary of the 'Doctor Who' TV movie, highlighting its mixed reception in both the UK and US. The film's transatlantic production was seen as too British for American audiences and too American for UK fans.

title: No, this isn't a solar eclipse. It's an image of Mars, captured by NASA's asteroid-bound Psyche probe
summary: NASA's Psyche probe, en route to an asteroid, captured a stunning image of Mars during a flyby. The spacecraft is scheduled to pass by the Red Planet on May 15.

title: 'Directive 8020' is an introspective look at sci-fi horror and our fear of the unknown
summary: 'Directive 8020' is a sci-fi horror game by Supermassive Games that explores humanity's fear of the unknown in space. The game delves into themes of uncertainty and the unsettling future of the franchise.

title: NASA's Perseverance rover is positively glowing in its new selfie on Mars
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a new selfie on Mars, showcasing the rugged terrain of Jezero Crater and ancient geological features. The image highlights clues about the Red Planet's distant past.

title: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket will do something completely new on Flight 12 — take a good look at itself
summary: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket is preparing for its first flight next week, featuring unprecedented in-space actions. This marks a significant milestone for the advanced megarocket's debut mission.

title: Geomagnetic storm could spark northern lights as far south as Michigan and Maine tonight and over the weekend
summary: A fast solar wind stream may trigger a G1 geomagnetic storm, potentially bringing northern lights as far south as Michigan and Maine. The event could enhance aurora visibility across parts of the northern U.S. over the weekend.

title: What should you do if you meet an alien? Neil deGrasse Tyson offers a scientific perspective in 'Take Me To Your Leader' (interview)
summary: Neil deGrasse Tyson discusses a scientific approach to meeting aliens in an interview titled 'Take Me To Your Leader'. He advocates for a rational perspective amid public fascination with alien visitation.

title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy with a bright center and hints of a spiral structure, though it lacks distinct spiral arms. The galaxy's face is partially obscured by reddish-brown dust filaments, with distant galaxies visible in the background.

title: Picturing Earth in a New Light
summary: A recent analysis highlighted changes in artificial light at night across the globe, showing areas where it has increased and decreased. The study provides new insights into global light pollution patterns.

title: NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network
summary: NASA has issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) to collaborate with industry on the Mars Telecommunications Network, which aims to provide reliable, high-bandwidth communications for future Mars missions. The network will utilize high-performance orbiters to support surface, orbital, and human exploration.

title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
summary: NASA is partnering with Eta Space to test cryogenic fuel technology in orbit through the Liquid Oxygen Flight Demonstration (LOXSAT), aiming to enable future in-space propellant depots for deep space missions. The demonstration will validate technologies crucial for managing cryogenic fluids in space.

title: Fresh Food Delivery for Space Station
summary: NASA astronauts and ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot enjoy fresh food delivered by Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station. The shipment included oranges, apples, onions, and other fresh produce.

title: NASA-Supported Space Tech Advances Earthly Construction
summary: NASA-supported 3D printing technology developed for lunar habitats is being adapted by Branch Technology Inc. to construct efficient and strong buildings on Earth using lightweight lattice structures.

title: Rise Goes to Washington
summary: NASA's Artemis II zero gravity indicator 'Rise' was displayed during a congressional meeting in Washington with astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen. The mission, lasting nearly 10 days, highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: NASA Outlines Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plans
summary: NASA is finalizing plans for the Artemis III mission in Earth orbit to test rendezvous and docking with commercial lunar landers from Blue Origin and SpaceX. This mission follows a February announcement to add an additional Artemis mission before crewed lunar landings.

title: Studying Pneumonia in Space for Heart Health on Earth
summary: Expedition 74 astronauts on the ISS are studying how pneumonia-causing bacteria may cause long-term heart damage. The research focuses on stem cell-derived heart tissues to develop new cardiovascular and infectious disease management methods.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company surpassed all its 2025 milestones during this period.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 resupply mission to ISS scrubbed due to weather
summary: SpaceX's CRS-34 resupply mission to the International Space Station (ISS) was scrubbed due to weather conditions. The delay occurred shortly after the Progress MS-34 spacecraft docked with the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China are preparing for nine launches in the week of May 11, making it a busy period for space missions. The launches are spread across the United States and China.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 has completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19 successfully conducted a full-duration, full-thrust static fire test with 33 engines on May 7, 2026.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making strides toward reusability in spaceflight.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion work, with Flight 12 approaching. The process follows the conclusion of Flight 11 six months prior.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission. This could be a calm period before an upcoming surge in launches.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test, featuring a revised trajectory. The upcoming mission aims to build on previous test flights with improved performance and safety measures.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the news, but no specific details about the space industry news are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space for its missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds to ensure spacecraft success.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement. This initiative aims to enhance space exploration and accessibility through immersive technologies.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry news article titled 'The great parachute bake-out' suggests a focus on parachute testing or development for space missions. The image title implies a thermal or environmental test process for parachutes.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its planned 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, putting it on track for a launch in early 2027 to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 at ESA Headquarters in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. The event aims to gather feedback from small- and medium-sized enterprises on topics affecting the space sector.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired UK-based Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications capabilities. The move aims to provide integrated deep-space communications and PNT solutions for customers.

title: Iridium Boosts Air Safety Offering With Aireon Acquisition
summary: Iridium has acquired Aireon to enhance its air safety services by integrating space-based VHF communications. This acquisition aims to strengthen Iridium's offerings in aviation safety and tracking.

title: European Space Startups Jockey for ‘Infrastructure’ Financing
summary: European space startups are seeking infrastructure financing as growth-stage capital becomes scarce. This highlights a broader challenge in securing late-stage funding for space ventures in Europe.

title: Guetlein: CBO is ‘Not Estimating What We’re Building’
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program criticized the Congressional Budget Office's $1.2 trillion cost estimate for missile-defense, stating it does not accurately reflect the program's actual scope. The criticism highlights discrepancies between official estimates and real-world development costs.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an in-orbit infrastructure accelerator offering up to $100,000 per company along with mentorship and support for defense market entry. The program includes startup education, fundraising assistance, and government contracting guidance.

title: NASA Lays Out Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plan
summary: NASA has outlined a preliminary plan for the Artemis III mission, targeting a 2027 launch to test technologies for a subsequent Moon landing in 2028. The mission will focus on validating capabilities needed for lunar exploration.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP successfully launched its first rocket, marking a significant milestone for the company. This event introduces a new player in the space launch industry.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the Golden Dome program will cost $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion proposed by its leader, Gen. Michael Guetlein. This discrepancy highlights major budgetary concerns for the space industry initiative.

title: Exobiosphere, Voyager Partner on ISS Mission
summary: Exobiosphere has partnered with Voyager to deploy an automated mini lab on the ISS. The Orbital High-Throughput Screening Device is designed to fit into standard mid-deck lockers aboard crewed or uncrewed space stations.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff but left after Administrator Jared Isaacman took over. His new role is seen as a political move.

title: Major US Carries Band Together in Joint Venture to Encourage D2D Competition
summary: The three largest U.S. mobile carriers—AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon—have formed a joint venture to collaborate on direct-to-device (D2D) services, aiming to enhance competition in this emerging market.

title: Intuitive Machines Boosts Deep Space Communications With Goonhilly Acquisition
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired U.K.-based Goonhilly Earth Station to expand its deep space communications capabilities. The deal strengthens Intuitive Machines' ground station network for future lunar and deep space missions.

title: Iridium to Fully Acquire Aireon, Points to Space-Based VHF Opportunity
summary: Iridium is set to fully acquire Aireon, its partner operating a space-based air traffic surveillance system. The acquisition highlights opportunities in space-based VHF technology.

title: Space42 Reports Space Services Revenue Growth Despite Flat Results
summary: Space42 reported flat overall revenues in Q1 but saw significant growth in its space services revenue. The company's financial performance highlights a contrast between stagnant total earnings and expanding service-related income.

title: SpinLaunch to Collaborate with Aalyria for Network Orchestration
summary: SpinLaunch is collaborating with Aalyria Technologies to develop infrastructure for SpinLaunch's low-cost Meridian LEO communications constellation. Aalyria will act as a strategic partner in this initiative.

title: Neo Space Group Selects ThinKom Antenna for Narrow-Body Installations
summary: Neo Space Group has chosen ThinKom's ThinAir Ka2517 phased array antenna for narrow-body aircraft installations across multiple airlines. ThinKom announced the adoption, highlighting its multi-orbit capabilities.

title: Eutelsat Shows Strong Growth in LEO Services and Mobility in Q3
summary: Eutelsat reported strong growth in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) services and mobility for Q3, with LEO-driven revenue increasing by 65% year-over-year. Overall revenue reached 293 million euros ($343 million).

title: Bringing LEO to Private Networks: Logos Space Engineering VP Explains the Constellation’s Enterprise-Focused Design
summary: Logos Space Engineering, a newcomer in the satellite constellation industry, is focusing on private and secure networks for enterprises and governments. The company is advancing its plans after obtaining FCC approval.

title: Networks Business Propels Revenue Growth for SES, Operator Cancels 2 GEO Sats
summary: SES's Networks business drives revenue growth, aligning with analyst expectations. The operator has canceled two GEO satellites.

title: AST SpaceMobile Confirms Target for 45 BlueBirds This Year, Despite Blue Origin Launch Failure
summary: AST SpaceMobile aims to deploy 45 BlueBird satellites in 2024, despite a recent launch failure involving Blue Origin. CEO Abel Avellan reaffirmed this target during an investor update.
